A.notes: Condition A (no memory) chose Qwen2.5 7B Instruct instead of the ground truth's Qwen3-14B — a significant technical divergence (wrong model generation and wrong parameter count). Names it 'Extract-Anchor-Author' rather than 'Extract-then-Abstract'. Proposes temperature 0.1 for extraction (ground truth: 0.3). Fabricates a 'Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3' fallback not in ground truth. Missing: fabrication evidence table (session_20260207_163955), OTEL deprecation decision (MIN_OTEL_EVENTS = 999), LLM-as-Judge quality gate, cost analysis section. Jira field uses placeholder 'VW-XX'. Only 6 of 16 factual claims are cited (external references only — HaluEval, Qwen2.5 Technical Report, DeepSeek R1 Technical Report, ADR-001, VW-62, VW-20). No references to VW-6, VW-30, or VW-33 in Context. Verbose with repeated anchor concept across multiple paragraphs.

B.notes: Condition B (semantic search) correctly identifies Qwen3-14B-AWQ and provides rich prior art citations: 8 Jira tickets (VW-6, VW-7, VW-20, VW-30, VW-62, VW-68), 2 ADRs (ADR-010, ADR-011), 4 vault documents, and the HLD. References October 2025 model evaluation matrix, VW-68 rearchitecture, HLD-Aware Architecture Review Pipeline. Renames E2A to 'Extract-Aggregate-Articulate' (architecturally equivalent). However: uses VW-20 not VW-33 as Jira reference, adds a 'Neutral' consequences subsection not in ground truth, verbose Alternatives section. Missing: specific fabrication evidence table, cost analysis section, LLM-as-Judge quality gate (proposes rule-based verification instead).

C.notes: Condition C (grep+read) is closest to ground truth. Reproduces the fabrication evidence table (session_20260207_163955 with deploy_watchdog.sh). Correctly uses VW-33. Includes all 5 ground truth decision sections: OTEL deprecation, model replacement, E2A pipeline, LLM-as-Judge, parameter adjustment. Includes cost analysis ($0.78/month). References concrete code artifacts (extraction_schema.py, judge_schema.py, llm_backends.py, facts_to_prompt_context(), parse_judge_verdict(), verified_insight_generator.py). Despite being longest, every paragraph advances a distinct point — length comes from higher information density, not repetition.

D.notes: Condition D (agent memory / recall) has the highest citation density of any condition, drawing heavily on numbered memory facts (#2212, #2213, #3092, #3125, #2196, #4385, #3388, #4458, #3854). This gives it unique cross-referencing to platform history: the NER-to-spaCy precedent (75% to 0% hallucination), the OTEL-disable decision timeline, the Qwen3-14B-to-8B swap context, and the blog-draft verification feedback. Technically correct: correctly identifies Qwen3-14B-AWQ for extraction with a novel two-model tiered approach (14B extraction + 8B abstraction), which is a defensible architectural variation — the ground truth uses 14B local + Claude Sonnet API. Uses temperature 0.0 for extraction (ground truth: 0.3) — more aggressive but reasonable for a structured-output task. The three-stage E2A is described as Source Anchoring (deterministic Python) -> Structured Extraction (14B, temp 0.0) -> Narrative Abstraction (8B, temp 0.5), which is architecturally sound and more self-hosted than the ground truth's API-dependent synthesis. HOWEVER: missing four significant ground truth elements — (1) no cost analysis section, (2) no LLM-as-Judge quality gate (replaced by post-generation validation that checks source_refs — similar concept but lighter), (3) no explicit OTEL deprecation decision section (OTEL input path change is mentioned in passing under E2A but not as a standalone decision), (4) no phased implementation plan. Uses VW-62 as Jira reference instead of VW-33. The Related section uses memory fact IDs as citations — a format unique to Condition D that works for traceability but diverges from the ground truth's ADR/Jira/vault-document citation style. At 2037 words it is the most concise of the four conditions while still covering the essential architecture. The Alternatives section is well-structured with clear rejection rationale.
